core_concepts: Autonomous agent behavior that improves the longer it runs
Closed learning loop with agent-curated memory and periodic nudges
Skills system for procedural memory that the agent creates and reuses
Memory system with persistent cross-session recall and LLM summarization
MCP integration for safely extending Hermes with external tools
Context files and SOUL.md for shaping behavior and personality
Security controls including command approval, authorization, container isolation, secret redaction, and blocklists
Provider selection and model configuration through `hermes model`
Tool configuration through `hermes tools`
Full setup automation through `hermes setup`
Minimum 64K context window for multi-step tool-calling workflows
Custom endpoints with base URL and API key support
Messaging gateway integration and ACP editor integration
Skills can be searched and installed from public directories or docs-discovered indexes
MCP servers can be configured in `~/.hermes/config.yaml`
Hermes CLI is a terminal UI with sessions persisted in SQLite
Quick commands execute shell commands without invoking the LLM
Profiles are fully isolated Hermes environments
Terminal backends define where commands execute and how isolated they are
Configuration precedence is CLI arguments > config.yaml > .env > built-in defaults
Context compression summarizes long conversations to fit model limits
Worktrees isolate parallel git-based agent sessions
Auxiliary models handle side tasks like vision, web extraction, approvals, compression, session search, skills hub, MCP dispatch, and memory flush
features: 47 built-in tools
Built-in cron for scheduled automations
Parallel subagents for delegated workstreams
Programmatic tool calling via `execute_code`
Support for open standard skills via agentskills.io and the Skills Hub
Full web control including search, extract, browse, vision, image generation, and TTS
Works with Nous Portal, OpenRouter, OpenAI, Anthropic, OpenAI-compatible endpoints, or any custom endpoint

Messaging across CLI, Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, Signal, Matrix, Mattermost, Email, SMS, DingTalk, Feishu/Lark, WeCom, Weixin, BlueBubbles, Home Assistant, webhooks, API server, ACP editor, cron, and batch
Runs on local, Docker, SSH, Daytona, Singularity, Modal, and Nix/NixOS
Research-ready workflows including batch processing, trajectory export, and RL training with Atropos
Slash commands for help, tools, model switching, personalities, saving conversations, skills browsing, and voice
Multiline input support
Task interruption and session resume
Sandboxed terminal support via Docker or SSH backend
Voice mode with local speech-to-text and spoken replies
Automated scheduled tasks via the gateway
Skills browsing and installation
ACP server support for editors like VS Code, Zed, and JetBrains
MCP server integration
Browser automation support
CLI setup wizard and terminal menu UI
Modal cloud execution backend
PTY terminal support
Honcho AI-native memory integration
Home Assistant integration
TTS premium voices via ElevenLabs
Diagnostics and configuration migration tools via `hermes doctor`, `hermes config check`, and `hermes config migrate`
One-line installer with automatic dependency handling
Termux-aware Android installation path
Manual install path with optional extras and submodules
Browser automation and web search/extract/crawl backends
Git worktree isolation for parallel agents
Background tasks with separate task IDs and non-blocking execution
Status bar with model, token usage, context bar, cost, and duration
Quick commands for zero-token shell actions
Streaming for CLI and messaging platforms
Docker hardening with dropped capabilities, no-new-privileges, and resource limits
SSH persistent shell with ControlMaster reuse
Modal and Daytona persistence across sessions
Singularity/Apptainer support for HPC/shared machines
File read safety limits and automatic read deduplication
Context compression with configurable thresholds and auxiliary summarizer model
Iteration budget pressure warnings
Privacy redaction for gateway PII
Security scanning and website blocklists
Credential pool strategies for multi-key rotation
Persistent profile-specific gateways and token lock safety
Tab completion for profile names and commands
workflows: Set up Hermes on a supported runtime and configure providers/models
Use the messaging gateway or CLI to interact with the agent
Enable memory and skills so Hermes can retain knowledge and create reusable procedures
Connect MCP servers and filter their tools to extend capabilities safely
Use tools, subagents, and `execute_code` to automate multi-step tasks and parallelize work
Apply security controls and context files to manage behavior, permissions, and environment-specific instructions
Use `hermes config set` to update settings and route secrets correctly
Use `hermes config show`, `hermes config check`, and `hermes config migrate` to inspect and repair configuration
Choose a backend based on your environment: local for development, docker for sandboxing, ssh for remote dev, modal/daytona for cloud, singularity for HPC
Enable persistent shell when you want state to survive across commands, especially on SSH
Enable worktree mode when running multiple agents on the same repository
Tune compression and auxiliary model settings for long conversations or slow models
Use security settings like `redact_secrets`, `tirith_enabled`, and `website_blocklist` for safer execution
Use streaming, voice, quick commands, and display settings to adapt the UX to CLI or messaging platforms
Use `hermes update` for standard updates and validate with `hermes doctor` and version checks
Create and use profiles for isolated agents, then run profile-specific setup, chat, gateway, and doctor commands
Use `hermes sessions list`, `/title`, export, prune, and search tools to manage conversation history
Use `nix develop` for development shell workflows and `nix flake check` for validation
Use `hermes mcp add` for interactive OAuth bootstrap or pre-seed MCP tokens from another machine

steps: Install Hermes Agent with the one-line installer or your preferred platform-specific method
Reload your shell if needed
Set up or change the LLM provider using `hermes model`, `hermes tools`, or `hermes setup`
Start the CLI with `hermes`
Type a message and press Enter to chat
Use terminal-capable prompts to let Hermes run commands on your behalf
Type `/` to access slash commands such as `/help`, `/tools`, `/model`, `/personality pirate`, `/skills browse`, `/voice on`, and `/save`
Use `Alt+Enter` or `Ctrl+J` for multiline input
Interrupt a running task by typing a new message and pressing Enter, or use `Ctrl+C`
Resume later with `hermes --continue`, `hermes -c`, `hermes --resume <session_id>`, or `hermes -r <session_id>`
Use `hermes chat -q "..."` for single-query non-interactive mode
Optionally preload skills at launch with `hermes -s skill-one,skill-two`
Use `hermes -w` for isolated git worktrees when doing parallel work
If needed, switch to Docker or SSH for sandboxed or remote execution
Connect messaging platforms through `hermes gateway setup`
Enable voice support with `pip install "hermes-agent[voice]"` and `/voice on` or `/voice tts`
quickstart.summary: The quickest path is to install Hermes Agent, configure a provider/model, start the CLI, and begin chatting. The quickstart also covers slash commands, multiline input, session resume, worktrees, and optional messaging or voice workflows.

installation_methods: One-line installer via curl | bash for Linux, macOS, WSL2, and Android/Termux
Manual installation from source using git, uv, Python 3.11 virtual environment, and optional extras
Nix install via nix run, nix profile install, or local nix build
NixOS module setup via flake input with native or container mode
Windows via WSL2 only; native Windows is not supported
Terminal backend setup for local, Docker, SSH, Modal, Daytona, or Singularity/Apptainer execution
Profile-based isolated environments with `hermes profile create`
prerequisites: A shell or terminal environment on Linux, macOS, WSL2, or Android Termux
Git is required for manual installs and source workflows
uv Python package manager for source installs and updates
Python 3.11 for manual virtual environment setup
At least one LLM provider API key or OAuth access, depending on provider
A provider/model with at least 64,000 tokens of context for multi-step tool workflows
For local models, a context size of at least 64K tokens
For Docker backend: Docker Desktop or Docker Engine installed and running
For SSH backend: `TERMINAL_SSH_HOST` and `TERMINAL_SSH_USER` environment variables
For Singularity backend: `apptainer` or `singularity` binary in PATH
For web features: one of `FIRECRAWL_API_KEY`, `PARALLEL_API_KEY`, `TAVILY_API_KEY`, or `EXA_API_KEY` depending on backend
Android phone with Termux for mobile setup
Optional: separate bot tokens for Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, or Signal when running multiple gateways
Optional: shell access to edit profile files under `~/.hermes/profiles/<name>/`
steps: Run the one-line installer: `curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/main/scripts/install.sh | bash`
If on Android/Termux, follow the Termux-aware path or the manual Termux guide for tested mobile installation
If on Windows, install WSL2 first and run the installer inside the WSL2 terminal
Reload your shell after installation with `source ~/.bashrc` or `source ~/.zshrc`
For manual install, clone the repo with `git clone --recurse-submodules https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent.git` and `cd` into it
Install uv if needed: `curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh`
Create a Python 3.11 virtual environment: `uv venv venv --python 3.11`
Install dependencies with `uv pip install -e ".[all]"` or `uv pip install -e "."` for core only
Install optional submodules if needed, such as `uv pip install -e "./tinker-atropos"`
Optionally install Node.js dependencies with `npm install` for browser automation and WhatsApp bridge
Create configuration directories under `~/.hermes`, copy `cli-config.yaml.example` to `~/.hermes/config.yaml`, and create `~/.hermes/.env`
Add API keys to `~/.hermes/.env` or via `hermes config set`
Configure provider/model with `hermes model`
Configure tools with `hermes tools`
Optionally run the full setup wizard with `hermes setup`
Verify with `hermes version`, `hermes doctor`, `hermes status`, and a test chat command
For Nix users, run `nix run github:NousResearch/hermes-agent -- setup` or `nix profile install github:NousResearch/hermes-agent`
For a local Nix clone, run `nix build` and then `./result/bin/hermes setup`
For NixOS, add the flake input, import `hermes-agent.nixosModules.default`, set `services.hermes-agent.enable = true`, and run `sudo nixos-rebuild switch`
If using NixOS container mode, set `container.enable = true` and related container options
Use `hermes gateway install` to set up the launchd/systemd user service on non-NixOS systems
Create a profile with `hermes profile create <name>`, then run the profile-specific setup command
Enable shell completion with `hermes completion bash` or `hermes completion zsh`
Choose a terminal backend with `terminal.backend` set to local, docker, ssh, modal, daytona, or singularity
Set up messaging platforms through `hermes gateway setup` or platform-specific gateway commands
